description: Check the logged-in Avatar's identity, karma, wallet, and holons created in the current workspace session. Shows that OASIS IDE keeps a living, permanent record of everything you build — not just files in a folder.
notes: This is a read-only sequence. No mutations.
It is safe to run at any time — call it as a 'health check + orientation' at the start of a session.
The oasis_search_holons step scopes to holons tagged with oasis.ide.* metadata, which covers everything the IDE Composer creates (NPC holons, quest holons, chat sessions, etc.).

steps.description: Confirm ONODE is reachable before making identity queries.
notes: Returns { healthy: true } or an error.
If this fails, ONODE is not running. Check Settings > Integrations and verify OASIS_API_URL.

steps.description: Retrieve the logged-in Avatar's full profile: level, karma, wallet addresses, username, join date.
notes: Uses the JWT from the current IDE session — no credentials needed.
Returns { id, username, level, karmaScore, wallets: [...], createdDate, ... }.
If this returns 401, the session has expired — re-login in Settings > Avatar.

steps.description: Find all holons created by this Avatar in the current workspace session today.

parameters.{todayIsoDate}: ISO date string for today's date at midnight UTC, e.g. '2026-04-15T00:00:00Z'

notes: Returns an array of holons: [{ id, name, createdDate, metadata, ... }].
Covers chat session holons, NPC holons (from npc-with-voice flows), quest holons (from quest-chain flows), and any other holons the agent created.
Broaden the filter by removing createdAfter to see all-time history for this Avatar.
If the array is empty, no holons have been created from this IDE session yet — that is expected for a fresh workspace.

steps.description: Compose a human-readable summary from steps 2 and 3 output.
steps.output_format: Avatar summary card: username, level, karma score, wallet count, holons created today (count + names), and a one-sentence interpretation of what the session has produced.
notes: This step is for the agent to summarise — no tool call needed.
Example output: 'You are StellarCreator (Level 12, Karma 847). You have 2 wallets. Today you created 4 holons: Martinez NPC, Meridian Job quest arc, Meridian Plasma Rifle item, and 1 chat session.'

session_opener_prompt: Show me my current OASIS Avatar status. Use oasis_get_avatar_detail to get my level, karma score, and wallet addresses. Then use oasis_search_holons to find holons I have created in this workspace today (metadata key 'oasis.ide'). Summarise: who I am, what I have built today, and what my karma score is.
agent_prompt_summary: Call oasis_get_avatar_detail for identity + karma, then oasis_search_holons with metadataKey 'oasis.ide' and today's date to show what has been built this session.
